speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Wine, wine, wine! What service is

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: here? I think our fellows are asleep.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Where’s Cotus? My master calls

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: for him. Cotus!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: A goodly house. The feast smells well, but I

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Appear not like a guest.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: What would you have, friend?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Whence are you? Here’s no place for you. Pray, go

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: to the door.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I have deserved no better entertainment

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: In being Coriolanus.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Whence are you, sir?—Has the

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: porter his eyes in his head, that he gives entrance

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: to such companions?—Pray, get you out.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Away!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Away? Get you away.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Now th’ art troublesome.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Are you so brave? I’ll have you

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: talked with anon.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: What fellow’s this?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: A strange one as ever I looked on. I

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: cannot get him out o’ th’ house. Prithee, call my

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: master to him.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: What have you to do here, fellow?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Pray you, avoid the house.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Let me but stand. I will not hurt your

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: hearth.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: What are you?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: A gentleman.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: A marv’llous poor one.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: True, so I am.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Pray you, poor gentleman, take up

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: some other station. Here’s no place for you. Pray

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: you, avoid. Come.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Follow your function, go, and batten on

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: cold bits.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: What, you will not?—Prithee, tell

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: my master what a strange guest he has here.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And I shall.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Where dwell’st thou?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Under the canopy.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Under the canopy?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Ay.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Where’s that?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I’ th’ city of kites and crows.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: I’ th’ city of kites and crows? What

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: an ass it is! Then thou dwell’st with daws too?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: No, I serve not thy master.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: How, sir? Do you meddle with my

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: master?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Ay, ’tis an honester service than to meddle

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: with thy mistress. Thou prat’st and prat’st. Serve

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: with thy trencher. Hence!

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Where is this fellow?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Here, sir. I’d have beaten him like

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: a dog, but for disturbing the lords within.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Whence com’st thou? What wouldst thou?

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Thy name? Why speak’st not? Speak, man. What’s

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: thy name?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: If, Tullus,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Not yet thou know’st me, and seeing me, dost not

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Think me for the man I am, necessity

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Commands me name myself.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: What is thy name?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: A name unmusical to the Volscians’ ears

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And harsh in sound to thine.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Say, what’s thy name?

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Thou hast a grim appearance, and thy face

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Bears a command in ’t. Though thy tackle’s torn,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Thou show’st a noble vessel. What’s thy name?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Prepare thy brow to frown. Know’st thou me yet?

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I know thee not. Thy name?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: My name is Caius Martius, who hath done

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To thee particularly and to all the Volsces

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Great hurt and mischief; thereto witness may

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: My surname Coriolanus. The painful service,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The extreme dangers, and the drops of blood

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Shed for my thankless country are requited

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: But with that surname, a good memory

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And witness of the malice and displeasure

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Which thou shouldst bear me. Only that name remains.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The cruelty and envy of the people,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Permitted by our dastard nobles, who

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Have all forsook me, hath devoured the rest,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And suffered me by th’ voice of slaves to be

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Whooped out of Rome. Now this extremity

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Hath brought me to thy hearth, not out of hope—

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Mistake me not—to save my life; for if

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I had feared death, of all the men i’ th’ world

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I would have ’voided thee, but in mere spite,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To be full quit of those my banishers,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Stand I before thee here. Then if thou hast

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: A heart of wreak in thee, that wilt revenge

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Thine own particular wrongs and stop those maims

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Of shame seen through thy country, speed thee straight

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And make my misery serve thy turn. So use it

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That my revengeful services may prove

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: As benefits to thee, for I will fight

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Against my cankered country with the spleen

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Of all the under fiends. But if so be

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Thou dar’st not this, and that to prove more fortunes

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Thou ’rt tired, then, in a word, I also am

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Longer to live most weary, and present

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: My throat to thee and to thy ancient malice,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Which not to cut would show thee but a fool,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Since I have ever followed thee with hate,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Drawn tuns of blood out of thy country’s breast,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And cannot live but to thy shame, unless

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: It be to do thee service.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: O Martius, Martius,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Each word thou hast spoke hath weeded from my heart

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: A root of ancient envy. If Jupiter

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Should from yond cloud speak divine things

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And say ’tis true, I’d not believe them more

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Than thee, all-noble Martius. Let me twine

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Mine arms about that body, whereagainst

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: My grainèd ash an hundred times hath broke

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And scarred the moon with splinters.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Here I clip

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: The anvil of my sword and do contest

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: As hotly and as nobly with thy love

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: As ever in ambitious strength I did

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Contend against thy valor. Know thou first,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I loved the maid I married; never man

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Sighed truer breath. But that I see thee here,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Thou noble thing, more dances my rapt heart

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Than when I first my wedded mistress saw

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Bestride my threshold. Why, thou Mars, I tell thee

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: We have a power on foot, and I had purpose

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Once more to hew thy target from thy brawn

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Or lose mine arm for ’t. Thou hast beat me out

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Twelve several times, and I have nightly since

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Dreamt of encounters ’twixt thyself and me;

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: We have been down together in my sleep,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Unbuckling helms, fisting each other’s throat,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And waked half dead with nothing. Worthy Martius,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Had we no other quarrel else to Rome but that

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Thou art thence banished, we would muster all

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: From twelve to seventy and, pouring war

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Into the bowels of ungrateful Rome,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Like a bold flood o’erbear ’t. O, come, go in,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And take our friendly senators by th’ hands,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Who now are here, taking their leaves of me,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Who am prepared against your territories,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Though not for Rome itself.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: You bless me, gods!

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Therefore, most absolute sir, if thou wilt have

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: The leading of thine own revenges, take

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Th’ one half of my commission and set down—

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: As best thou art experienced, since thou know’st

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Thy country’s strength and weakness—thine own ways,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Whether to knock against the gates of Rome,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Or rudely visit them in parts remote

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: To fright them ere destroy. But come in.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Let me commend thee first to those that shall

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Say yea to thy desires. A thousand welcomes!

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: And more a friend than ere an enemy—

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Yet, Martius, that was much. Your hand. Most welcome!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Here’s a strange alteration!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: By my hand, I had thought to

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: have strucken him with a cudgel, and yet my mind

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: gave me his clothes made a false report of him.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: What an arm he has! He turned me

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: about with his finger and his thumb as one would

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: set up a top.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Nay, I knew by his face that there

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: was something in him. He had, sir, a kind of face,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: methought—I cannot tell how to term it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: He had so, looking as it were—

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Would I were hanged but I thought there was

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: more in him than I could think.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: So did I, I’ll be sworn. He is simply

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: the rarest man i’ th’ world.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: I think he is. But a greater soldier

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: than he you wot one.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Who, my master?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Nay, it’s no matter for that.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Worth six on him.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Nay, not so neither. But I take him

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: to be the greater soldier.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Faith, look you, one cannot tell

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: how to say that. For the defense of a town our general

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: is excellent.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Ay, and for an assault too.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: O slaves, I can tell you news, news,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: you rascals!

speaker: BOTH
text: What, what, what? Let’s partake!

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: I would not be a Roman, of all nations;

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: I had as lief be a condemned man.

speaker: BOTH
text: Wherefore? Wherefore?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Why, here’s he that was wont to

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: thwack our general, Caius Martius.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Why do you say “thwack our

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: general”?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: I do not say “thwack our general,”

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: but he was always good enough for him.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Come, we are fellows and friends.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: He was ever too hard for him; I have heard him

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: say so himself.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: He was too hard for him directly, to

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: say the truth on ’t, before Corioles; he scotched

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: him and notched him like a carbonado.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: An he had been cannibally given,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: he might have boiled and eaten him too.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: But, more of thy news.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Why, he is so made on here within

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: as if he were son and heir to Mars; set at upper end

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: o’ th’ table; no question asked him by any of the

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: senators but they stand bald before him. Our general

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: himself makes a mistress of him, sanctifies

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: himself with ’s hand, and turns up the white o’ th’

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: eye to his discourse. But the bottom of the news is,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: our general is cut i’ th’ middle and but one half of

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: what he was yesterday, for the other has half, by

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: the entreaty and grant of the whole table. He’ll go,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: he says, and sowl the porter of Rome gates by th’

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: ears. He will mow all down before him and leave

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: his passage polled.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And he’s as like to do ’t as any

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: man I can imagine.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Do ’t? He will do ’t! For, look you,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: sir, he has as many friends as enemies, which

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: friends, sir, as it were, durst not, look you, sir, show

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: themselves, as we term it, his friends whilest he’s

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: in directitude.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Directitude? What’s that?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: But when they shall see, sir, his

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: crest up again, and the man in blood, they will out

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: of their burrows like coneys after rain, and revel

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: all with him.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: But when goes this forward?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Tomorrow, today, presently. You

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: shall have the drum struck up this afternoon. ’Tis,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: as it were, a parcel of their feast, and to be executed

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: ere they wipe their lips.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Why then, we shall have a stirring

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: world again. This peace is nothing but to rust iron,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: increase tailors, and breed ballad-makers.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Let me have war, say I. It exceeds

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: peace as far as day does night. It’s sprightly walking,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: audible, and full of vent. Peace is a very apoplexy,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: lethargy; mulled, deaf, sleepy, insensible; a getter

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: of more bastard children than war’s a destroyer of

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: men.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: ’Tis so, and as wars in some sort

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: may be said to be a ravisher, so it cannot be denied

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: but peace is a great maker of cuckolds.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Ay, and it makes men hate one

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: another.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Reason: because they then less

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: need one another. The wars for my money! I hope

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: to see Romans as cheap as Volscians.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: They are rising; they are rising.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              AND
               
              SECOND
               
              SERVINGMEN
text: In, in, in, in!
